title: 10 best herbal tinctures for heartburn

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach. The glycyrrhizin in licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation and protect against acid reflux, allowing the body to naturally heal and recover from heartburn episodes. Additionally, licorice tinctures have been found to increase the production of mucus, which helps to coat and shield the lining of the esophagus, further reducing discomfort and irritation associated with heartburn.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by heartburn.

Liquiritin: This flavonoid has been shown to inhibit the production of digestive enzymes that contribute to heartburn symptoms, such as pepsin and gastric acid, thereby reducing the severity of heartburn episodes.

Aminosteroids: These compounds have been found to relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), which can help prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and reduce heartburn symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizic acid: This triterpenoid saponin helps with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, and also has a protective effect on the mucous membranes.
Licoricidin: This phenolic glycoside has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the inflammation and irritation associated with heartburn, thereby providing relief from symptoms.
Liquiritigenin: This isoflavone has a protective effect on the gastric mucosa, helping to prevent damage and inflammation caused by stomach acid, which is often a contributing factor to heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and store in a clean glass container.
Combine the roots with 750ml of 70% ethanol in a dark glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with heartburn because of its natural ability to relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES) muscle, which prevents stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing discomfort. The menthol content in peppermint also has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, further alleviating symptoms of heartburn. By soothing and calming the digestive tract, herbal peppermint tinctures provide relief from heartburn and promote overall digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating discomfort associated with heartburn.

Soothing gastroprotective properties: Peppermint's glycerol esters and flavonoids work together to protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing acid reflux and irritation caused by heartburn.
Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint's volatile oils, including menthol and limonene, help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, easing cramps and spasms that can exacerbate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as an antispasmodic and local anesthetic, providing relief from heartburn by reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to neutralize stomach acid and protect the esophageal lining from damage.
Menthone: Has antispasmodic and carminative effects, which help to ease digestion and relieve symptoms of heartburn by reducing spasms in the stomach and intestines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide quick relief from heartburn symptoms.
Stems: They contain a smaller amount of menthol and menthone compared to leaves, but can still contribute to the tincture's effectiveness in alleviating heartburn.
Roots: They have a higher concentration of menthol and menthone than stems, making them a popular alternative to leaves for heartburn relief in some herbal remedies.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 pound of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and stems, and chop them finely.
Combine the chopped herb with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with heartburn because its natural anti-inflammatory properties soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The volatile oils in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to relax the esophageal sphincter and reduce acid reflux. Additionally, ginger's carminative properties help eliminate gas and bloating, further alleviating symptoms of heartburn. As a natural remedy, herbal ginger tinctures offer a gentle and effective way to manage heartburn and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate heartburn symptoms.

Digestive aid property: Ginger's natural digestive enzymes stimulate digestion and improve gastric motility, helping to reduce symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn by allowing food to pass through the digestive system more efficiently.
Antispasmodic property: Ginger's antispasmodic compounds relax the muscles in the esophagus and stomach, reducing spasms that can contribute to heartburn and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help alleviate heartburn by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, which in turn prevents stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
Shogaols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

Volatile oils (particularly Zingiberene): These oils have a calming effect on the digestive system, which helps to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux, promoting a smooth flow of food through the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome is the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for making tinctures due to its high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make tinctures because they contain a similar composition to the rhizome, providing similar benefits for heartburn relief.

Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are occasionally used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that may help soothe and protect the digestive system, potentially alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Combine the chopped rhizomes with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for 2 to 3 weeks shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with heartburn because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins that work together to soothe and calm the esophageal lining. These compounds help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the esophagus, allowing for easier digestion and reducing the likelihood of stomach acid flowing back up into the esophagus, thereby alleviating symptoms of heartburn. Additionally, maypop's natural antacid properties also help neutralize excess stomach acid, providing fast relief from heartburn discomfort.
remedy_properties: Alkaloids: Maypop tincture contains alkaloids, such as pseudopelletierine and pseudophrydain, which have been shown to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Tannins: The high concentration of tannins in maypop tincture helps to reduce the production of stomach acid, thus decreasing the likelihood of heartburn episodes and alleviating discomfort.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in maypop tincture have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the mucous membranes lining the esophagus, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by stomach acid reflux.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: Harmane is a beta-carboline alkaloid that may help alleviate heartburn by reducing the sensitivity of the stomach lining and preventing the release of stomach acid.
Isovitexin: Isovitexin is a flavonoid glycoside that exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Passifloric acid: Passifloric acid is a phenolic compound that has been shown to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, reducing the risk of acid reflux and heartburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Passiflora incarnata for heartburn remedies due to their high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems contain a higher concentration of passifloric acid, which is known for its ability to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, relieving heartburn symptoms.

Fruits: The fruits are also used to make tinctures for heartburn, as they contain a combination of flavonoids, alkaloids, and sugars that help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in late summer when they are in full bloom for six weeks.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a single layer on paper bags at room temperature for two weeks.
Combine one part dried flowers and leaves with two parts 80 proof vodka or 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with heartburn because its mucilaginous properties form a protective barrier in the esophagus, soothing irritated tissues and reducing inflammation. The saponins present in marshmallow root also have a calming effect on the digestive tract, relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter and preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. As a result, heartburn symptoms such as burning sensations and discomfort are alleviated, allowing for more comfortable digestion and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow mucilage forms a protective barrier on the esophageal lining, soothing and coating any irritated areas to alleviate heartburn symptoms. This helps reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by acid reflux.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow tincture help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can exacerbate heartburn symptoms. By reducing inflammation, marshmallow tincture can provide relief from chest pain and discomfort associated with acid reflux.

Soothing demulcents: Marshmallow tincture's soothing demulcent properties calm and protect the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, which helps reduce irritation and inflammation caused by stomach acid backing up into the esophagus.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages, such as glycoproteins and polysaccharides, help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.

Althaea root flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Althaea officinalis, such as quercetin and kaempferol, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate heartburn symptoms by reducing inflammation and protecting the mucous membranes.

Saponins: Saponins in Althaea officinalis have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help calm the digestive tract and reduce the frequency and severity of heartburn episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are used to make tinctures for heartburn because they contain mucilages that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
Leaves: Althaea officinalis leaves are used to make tinctures for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Stems: Althaea officinalis stems are used to make tinctures for heartburn because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of clean fresh or dried roots of this plant in the morning when it's free of moisture.
Chop the roots into small pieces to release their active compounds and increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 80-100 ml of 25% glycerin in a glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow the roots to infuse the glycerin.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with heartburn because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The plant's bitter compounds stimulate digestive enzymes, helping to break down food more efficiently and reduce acidity in the stomach. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate irritation and inflammation in the esophagus, alleviating discomfort and burning sensations associated with heartburn. By promoting healthy digestion and reducing inflammation, dandelion tinctures provide natural relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Bitter compounds: Dandelion's bitter compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, stimulate digestive enzymes and improve digestion, reducing inflammation and irritation in the esophagus that can contribute to heartburn.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's high antioxidant content helps protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach and esophagus from oxidative stress and damage caused by acid reflux, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the esophageal lining, alleviating heartburn symptoms and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as chlorogenic acid, have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, potentially soothing the stomach lining and reducing acid production, which may contribute to heartburn relief.
Inulin: Inulin, a type of fructan, may help alleviate heartburn by regulating gut bacteria, which can contribute to digestive issues, and promoting a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for heartburn tinctures due to their high mucilage content, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus.
Roots: Utilized for heartburn tinctures because they contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that can help regulate gut bacteria and alleviate digestive issues.
Flowers: Employed in heartburn tinctures due to their bitter properties, which can stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility to reduce heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in equal proportions and dry them in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours.
Crush 200g of dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine the powder with 50% ethanol in a 1:2 ratio to create the tincture mixture.
Steep the tincture mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterbur tinctures helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The herb contains compounds that reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn. Additionally, butterbur's relaxant effects on smooth muscle tissue can also help to reduce spasms in the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acids from flowing back up into the esophagus and providing relief from heartburn discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Butterbur's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as petasin and isopetasin, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of heartburn.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in butterbur tinctures relax the muscles in the esophageal sphincter, preventing excessive contractions that can cause acid reflux and heartburn.

Soothing and protective mucosal effects: Butterbur's flavonoids and terpenoids help soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach and esophagus, reducing irritation and damage caused by stomach acids.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help alleviate heartburn symptoms by relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter and reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach.
Petasine: This terpene constituent has anti-inflammatory and relaxant properties, which help to soothe the esophagus and reduce symptoms of heartburn, such as acid reflux and inflammation.

Harpagide: This iridoid glycoside has been shown to have a protective effect on the esophageal mucosa, reducing inflammation and preventing damage caused by acid reflux, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most commonly used part for heartburn tinctures because they contain the highest concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are also commonly used due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Barks: Barks are occasionally used because they contain some amount of sesquiterpenes, although in smaller quantities compared to roots and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petasites hybridus leaves and roots in early spring or fall when the plant is dormant for 30 minutes.
Clean and dry the plant material by spreading it out in a single layer for 2 days.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter for 10 minutes.
Steep the chopped plant material in 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio for 2 weeks.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with heartburn because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The mucilages present in aloe vera gel form a protective barrier against stomach acid, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn. Additionally, aloe's natural antimicrobial agents inhibit the growth of bacteria and yeast that can contribute to heartburn. By promoting a healthy gut environment, aloe tinctures alleviate symptoms of heartburn, providing relief from burning sensations and discomfort in the chest and throat.
remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Aloe vera's natural compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help to reduce inflammation and soothe the esophageal lining, providing rapid relief from heartburn symptoms.

Antioxidant Activity: Aloe vera's rich antioxidant profile helps to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage to the esophageal mucosa, reducing the risk of acid reflux and heartburn.

Prostaglandin Modulation: Aloe vera's prostaglandin-modulating properties help to regulate the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that play a role in the contraction and relaxation of smooth muscle in the esophagus, thereby reducing spasms and discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones, specifically aloe-emodin, help with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and promoting the healing of esophageal lesions, which can cause irritation and discomfort.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, help with heartburn by reducing inflammation and protecting the esophagus from oxidative damage, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms.
Saponins: Saponins, particularly aloin and aloe-catechin, help with heartburn by soothing the mucous membranes in the esophagus and reducing the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera for heartburn tinctures due to their high gel content and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems are also used for heartburn tinctures, as they contain aloe-emodin, a compound that helps soothe digestive issues.
Buds: The buds are another part used for heartburn tinctures, containing a mixture of compounds that aid in digestion and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100% organic aloe vera leaves with thick green skin for tincture making.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and weigh out 250 grams for tincturing.
Combine the chopped aloe vera with 750 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the aloe vera mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with heartburn because it contains potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by stomach acid reflux. The tannins present in elder flowers also help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and alleviating symptoms of heartburn. remedy_properties: Antacid and anti-inflammatory effects: Elder tincture helps neutralize stomach acid and reduces inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.

remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms.

Fruits: The fruits of Sambucus nigra, also known as elderberries, have been used to treat heartburn and other digestive problems due to their high antioxidant content and ability to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh flowers of the plant in question in early summer morning.
Dry the fresh flowers in a warm dark place for 1 to 2 weeks.
Combine 1 part of the dried flowers with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle.

These potent antioxidants soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause acid reflux and discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest crataegus monogyna leaves and twigs at dawn when dew is present and dry them thoroughly within 2 days.
Crush dried crataegus monogyna plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the crushed plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking it every 2 days to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

Combine 250ml of high-proof vodka with 1:5 ratio of chopped plant material to solvent steeping the mixture in a glass jar for 14 days.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh viscum album leaves and stems in their growing season ensuring sufficient quantity for the desired yield.
Clean and dry the harvested material by gently brushing off dirt and debris with a soft-bristled brush taking 30 minutes.
Chop the dried material into smaller pieces using scissors or a herb cutter to increase surface area facilitating extraction.
Combine chopped viscum album with vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a glass jar at a ratio of one part herb to two parts solvent steeping for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids and storing the liquid in dark glass bottles for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with heartburn because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort. remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds and transfer them to a clean glass jar.
Add one cup of high-proof vodka or brandy to the jar and stir well to combine ingredients.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Label and store the final tincture in a cool dark place at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of cumin contain compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive tract, making them effective in treating heartburn symptoms.
Seeds: Cumin seeds have a natural anti-inflammatory property that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and relieve discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coriandrum sativum leaves and dry them using shade drying or dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Grind dried coriandrum sativum into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix one part powdered coriandrum sativum with three parts vodka in a glass jar and store it at room temperature for 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar and discard solids after 1 week.
Bottle and label the tincture, taking 20 drops sublingually 3 times daily as needed for heartburn relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander tinctures helps with heartburn because it contains compounds that have a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and relieving discomfort. The essential oils in coriander have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the esophageal mucous membranes, reducing irritation and pain associated with heartburn. Additionally, coriander's carminative properties help to expel gas and ease digestion, further alleviating symptoms of heartburn.
remedy_properties: Antacid and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Coriander tincture helps to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.

Carminative Properties: Coriander's natural carminative properties help to release trapped gas in the digestive tract, reducing pressure on the lower esophageal sphincter and alleviating heartburn discomfort.

Antioxidant Properties: Coriander tincture is rich in antioxidants that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excess acid and inflammation, promoting healing and preventing further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of coriander are often used in herbal remedies for heartburn because they have been traditionally shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm digestive issues.
Seeds: Coriander seeds are commonly used to make tea or tincture, as they contain compounds that have been shown to relax the muscles in the esophagus and relieve symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coriander leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has dried.
Weigh out 500 grams of freshly harvested coriander plant material into a clean glass jar.
Combine the coriander plant material with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in the jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 2 minutes each time.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle and store it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Agrimony tinctures helps with heartburn because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Its bitter constituents also help stimulate digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing the risk of acid reflux. Additionally, agrimony's antispasmodic effects can relax the muscles in the esophagus and stomach, easing symptoms of heartburn and indigestion. By addressing these underlying issues, agrimony tinctures provide natural relief from heartburn discomfort, promoting a healthier digestive system overall.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Agrimony tincture's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of heartburn such as discomfort and pain.

Gastroprotective properties: The herb's gastroprotective properties can protect the mucous membranes of the esophagus and stomach from acid damage, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by heartburn.

Antioxidant properties: Agrimony tincture's antioxidant properties can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress in the digestive tract, which can exacerbate heartburn symptoms and damage to the esophageal lining.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Agrimony are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing heartburn symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Agrimony contain glycosides and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried agrimonia eupatoria leaves and flowers measuring one part herb to two parts menstruum.
Combine the herb mixture with vodka or neutral grain spirit at a ratio of 1:2 by weight in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking it daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids after one hour.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a refrigerator at 40°f for up to two years, dosing one quarter teaspoon orally three times daily.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Kansas Coneflower's antioxidants help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation, alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The plant's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as alkamides and lignans, soothe the digestive tract and reduce irritation, providing relief from heartburn discomfort.

Barks: The barks of the Kansas coneflower contain a significant amount of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenes, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia flowers at the peak of blooming and sift through for any debris.
Chop 50-60 grams of echinacea angustifolia flowers into smaller pieces to increase surface area.
Combine chopped echinacea flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily, allowing for extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with heartburn because its active compound, triterpenoid saponins, has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach. This natural remedy also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and heal any damage caused by acid reflux. By promoting a healthy balance of digestive enzymes and reducing inflammation, Kansas coneflower tinctures may provide relief from heartburn symptoms, allowing for more comfortable digestion and overall well-being.

Silver daisy tinctures helps with heartburn because it contains antacid properties that help neutralize stomach acid, providing quick relief from burning sensations in the chest and throat. The natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds found in silver daisy also soothe the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux. Additionally, silver daisy's ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract helps prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus, providing long-lasting relief from heartburn symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ursinia anthemoides leaves and flowers, ensuring at least 50g of plant material.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces, then mix with 80% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to extract essential oils and compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids and preserving the liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, using within one year of preparation and dosing at 20-30 drops three times daily.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for heartburn are concentrated liquid extracts of plant-based remedies that help alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux by soothing the stomach lining and reducing inflammation. The best herbal tinctures for heartburn include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Mentha x piperita, Alpinia galanga, Cinchona officinalis, Zanthoxylum bungeanum, Aloe ferox, Lavandula angustifolia, and Aloe barbadensis. If left untreated, heartburn can disrupt daily life, causing discomfort, digestive issues, and even social anxiety, leading to a decreased quality of life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to heartburn that are treated with herbal tinctures are acid reflux, indigestion, and dyspepsia. Herbal remedies such as gentian root, chamomile, and licorice root are often used to soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms like bloating, discomfort, and burning sensations in the chest and throat. These herbs work by reducing inflammation, calming the stomach lining, and improving digestion.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with heartburn is a blend of Dandelion root, Peppermint, and Ginger. Dandelion root supports digestive health and reduces inflammation in the stomach, while Peppermint helps to relax the muscles and reduce acid production. Ginger, with its natural anti-inflammatory properties, aids in soothing the esophagus and calming the digestive system. This combination can help alleviate heartburn symptoms and promote a balanced digestive system.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine (New York, N.Y.), herbal tinctures are good for heartburn because they alleviate symptoms related to gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) through various mechanisms such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, downregulation of genes encoding inflammatory proteins, improvement of barrier function and gastric mucus, decrease in gastric acid, and induction of tonic contractions of the lower esophageal sphincter. The study included 13 human studies, which found that all herbal preparations used led to the alleviation of symptoms related to GERD, with some herbal medicines, such as Myrtus communis and Cydonia oblonga, showing a marked reduction in GERD symptoms comparable to omeprazole. The study suggests that the multiple mechanisms of action of medicinal plants in GERD provide more efficient treatment and help to manage histopathological changes associated with this disorder.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and alleviating discomfort associated with heartburn. Additionally, the warming properties of ginger stimulate digestion and promote the relaxation of the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing heartburn symptoms to subside. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for heartburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms.
Shogaols: Shogaols have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate heartburn by reducing the production of stomach acid and improving digestion.

Volatile oils: These oils, particularly the sesquiterpene and monoterpenoid compounds, help with heartburn by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing spasms that can contribute to heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part, as they are the source of ginger's medicinal compounds and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe heartburn symptoms.
Roots: The roots are often used in combination with the rhyzomes and contain similar medicinal compounds, contributing to their effectiveness in heartburn tinctures.

Stems: The stems may also be used to make tinctures, although they are less commonly used than the rhyzomes and roots, as they still contain some medicinal compounds that help alleviate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh root material when it's mature and the rhizomes are firm and free from mold.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces using a sharp tool and wear protective gloves.
Combine the chopped material with 80-100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar in a 1:5 ratio.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional gentle shaking and stirring.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with heartburn because of its soothing properties that calm the digestive system. The licorice root extract in these tinctures has anti-inflammatory effects that reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating the burning sensation associated with heartburn. Additionally, glycyrrhizin, a key compound in Glycyrrhiza glabra, helps to neutralize stomach acid and promote a healthy gut environment, making it easier to manage heartburn symptoms. This natural remedy provides relief for those struggling with heartburn and digestive discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Glycyrrhetic acid: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect the esophageal mucosa and reduce acid reflux.
Licoricidin (a type of flavonoid): It has a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used to make licorice root tinctures due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which helps soothe heartburn symptoms.
Barks: The bark of Glycyrrhiza glabra is also used to make tinctures, as it contains glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate heartburn.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are sometimes used to make tinctures, as they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin and other compounds that contribute to the plant's anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra at the optimal time to ensure potency and quality.
Chop the roots into smaller pieces to increase the surface area for solvent penetration and extraction.
Steep 250ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass container with the chopped roots for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to separate the liquid from the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dark place.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves are commonly used to make tinctures for heartburn due to their high concentration of volatile oils that help alleviate digestive issues.

Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are used to make tinctures for heartburn because they contain a compound called anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive system.

remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems when they are fresh and at their peak potency in mid-summer.
Chop the foeniculum vulgare plant material into small pieces to ensure maximum extraction in the next step.
Combine 1 part foeniculum vulgare material with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar occasionally.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tinctures helps with heartburn because it contains compounds like menthol and limonene that have natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of mentha x piperita when they are in full growth and at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours or air dry them.
Combine dried leaves with 60 percent vodka as a solvent in a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a glass container with a lid for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid from the solids using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe ferox tinctures helps with heartburn because of its soothing and protective properties. remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These help with heartburn by soothing and protecting the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation.
Anthraquinones: These help with heartburn by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and inflammation, and promoting the healing of mucous membranes.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most used part due to their high concentration of anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Barks: The barks are also used due to their content of anthraquinones and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, providing relief from heartburn.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of aloe ferox leaves from a mature plant, cut into small pieces, and dry them in a dehydrator at 50c for 24 hours.
Combine the dried aloe ferox pieces with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar, ensuring a 1:5 ratio.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures helps with heartburn because the calming properties of the lavender plant, specifically its essential oils, can soothe and relax the muscles in the digestive tract. remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain volatile oils that help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with heartburn because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel from the aloe vera plant contains mucilages, which form a protective barrier on the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux. This natural barrier helps to calm the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as burning sensations and discomfort associated with heartburn. Regular use of aloe barbadensis tinctures can provide long-term relief and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannan: This polysaccharide helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
Acemannan: This complex polysaccharide has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating heartburn symptoms.

Aloin: This anthraquinone glycoside has a relaxing effect on the muscles of the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and relieving heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves of Aloe barbadensis is often used to create a tincture due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Barks: Aloe barbadensis bark tincture is sometimes used to treat heartburn due to its ability to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote healing in the esophagus.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe barbadensis leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated for best results.
Dry the harvested aloe barbadensis leaves in a single layer at 35-40°c for 24-48 hours.
Crush the dried aloe barbadensis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Combine 1 part aloe barbadensis powder with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar for a 10:20 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
